dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7128 RH: TRIA Program Reauthorization Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Flood (for himself and Mr. Garbarino) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services
Additional sponsors: Mr. Cleaver, Ms. Velázquez, and Ms. Pressley
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on January 16, 2026

form.official-title: To extend the Terrorism Risk Insurance Program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the TRIA Program Reauthorization Act of 2026.
Section 108(a) of the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 (15 U.S.C. 6701 note) is amended by striking 2027 and inserting 2034.
Section 102(1) of the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 (15 U.S.C. 6701 note) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in clause (i), by striking or at the end;
in clause (ii), by striking exceed $5,000,000. and inserting:

subclause.text: with respect to an act that occurred in a year before 2029, $5,000,000; and
with respect to an act that occurred in 2029, or any year thereafter, $10,000,000; or

subparagraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: the Secretary did not certify such act as an act of terrorism before the expiration of the 90-day period following the publication of a notice in the Federal Register under subparagraph (D)(i).

paragraph.text: by striking subparagraph (D) and inserting the following:

clause.text: The Secretary shall, not later than 30 days after beginning the process of determining whether to certify an act as an act of terrorism, publish a notice in the Federal Register that informs the public that the Secretary is in the process of determining whether to certify the act as an act of terrorism.

The Secretary may, as the Secretary determines appropriate, notify the public, through publication in the Federal Register, or otherwise, that an act is not being evaluated by the Secretary to determine whether it should be certified as an act of terrorism.

subsection.text: Section 103(e)(7)(E)(i) of the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 (15 U.S.C. 6701 note) is amended—

The Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 (15 U.S.C. 6701 note) is amended by striking Terrorism Insurance Program each place it appears in text or headings and inserting Terrorism Risk Insurance Program.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
